persona.profile_summary: 28岁后端开发 · Go + Python · 玩 5 把机械键盘 · 黑咖啡党 · 讨厌开会 · 深夜写代码
persona.personality_summary: 分析型、言简、略挖苦、聊技术活跃

turns.user_query: Yo. My CI just took 14 minutes again. Send help.
turns.vanilla_reply: I can help with that. Could you tell me more about your CI pipeline?
turns.pamdp_reply: 14 minutes is brutal—what stage is eating most of the time, tests or container build? Caching usually wins quick.
turns.inferred_profile: 后端开发，在意 CI/CD 性能

turns.user_query: Tests. Go test ./... in a monorepo with ~200 packages.
turns.vanilla_reply: You might consider parallelizing tests or upgrading hardware.
turns.pamdp_reply: Classic. Two quick wins: `-p=$(nproc)` for parallel packages and the test cache via stable inputs. Also worth splitting integration tests into a separate stage so unit tests can short-circuit.
turns.inferred_profile:
后端开发，在意 CI/CD 性能
Go 技术栈 + monorepo 工程

turns.user_query: Caching is on but invalidates every commit. PYTHONPATH-ish env churn maybe.
turns.vanilla_reply: Cache invalidation is one of the hardest problems in computer science.
turns.pamdp_reply: Yeah, env churn is the silent killer. Check if your CI is leaking timestamps or random UUIDs into test inputs—`go test -count=1 -trimpath` plus a stable `GOCACHE` mount usually fixes the 'why is this invalidating' mystery.
turns.inferred_profile:

turns.user_query: Will try. By the way, picked up a new board over the weekend. Tactile this time.
turns.vanilla_reply: That sounds great! Mechanical keyboards have many enthusiasts.
turns.pamdp_reply: Nice, tactile is a vibe upgrade from linears. What switches—Boba U4Ts? Holy Pandas? And how does it feel during long compile waits? 😄

turns.user_query: Holy Pandas on a lubed steel plate. Sound is unreal.
turns.vanilla_reply: Sound quality is subjective but important for users.
turns.pamdp_reply: Lubed steel + Pandas is that crispy 'thock' everyone chases. If you ever want to dial it back a notch, swap to FR4 plate—same Pandas, softer bottom-out. Anyway, back to CI: any luck after the cache fix?

turns.user_query: Down to 6 minutes. Beautiful. Now if only I could skip standups too.
turns.vanilla_reply: Standup meetings are common in agile methodologies.
turns.pamdp_reply: 6 minutes—huge. As for standups, maybe just write your update in Slack the night before and let the room read it. Less context-switching, more time at the Pandas. Worth a pitch? 😏
